3	\chapter{Coheight--Krull dim bridge for scheme points}
4	\label{chap:Albanese_CoheightBridge}
5	
6	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-183). This chapter is a project-side scaffold sitting
7	% between \cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension} and the Riemann--Roch
8	% Weil-divisor machinery of \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}. The
9	% directive originates from the iter-182 plan-phase api-alignment consult
10	% \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} (Decision 2), which determined
11	% that Mathlib at the project's pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) ships every
12	% prerequisite for the scheme-level coheight \(=\) Krull-dim-of-stalk
13	% identification but does \emph{not} ship the bridge itself. The bridge is
14	% \emph{not} a published-textbook lemma; it is the project-side assembly of
15	% four standard Mathlib API pieces:
16	%   1. \texttt{Order.coheight\_orderIso} -- coheight transport across order
17	%      isomorphisms (Mathlib \texttt{Order.KrullDimension}).
18	%   2. \texttt{Topology.Inseparable.Specializes.mem\_open} -- opens contain
19	%      every generisation of their points (Mathlib \texttt{Topology.Inseparable}).
20	%   3. \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height} -- the algebra
21	%      bridge between localisation Krull dim and prime-ideal height
22	%      (Mathlib \texttt{RingTheory.Ideal.Height}).
23	%   4. \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk} -- the affine-stalk
24	%      \(=\) localisation identification (Mathlib \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}).
25	% The Lean target file \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean}
26	% does not yet exist; this chapter is the prover-ready specification for
27	% creating it. None of the four blocks below has a direct Hartshorne / Milne /
28	% Mumford / Stacks tag that quotes the exact statement, so the blocks are
29	% packaged as Archon-original assembly lemmas with explicit Mathlib API
30	% pointers in lieu of \texttt{\% SOURCE QUOTE:} blocks.
31	
32	\section{Setup and motivation}
33	\label{sec:coheight_setup}
34	
35	A point \(z\) of a scheme \(X\) has two natural ``dimension'' invariants attached
36	to it. The \emph{Krull dimension of the local ring at \(z\)}, written
37	\(\dim \mathcal{O}_{X,z}\), is an algebraic quantity: the supremum of the lengths
38	of chains of prime ideals in the stalk \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\). The
39	\emph{coheight} of \(z\) in \(X\), written \(\operatorname{coheight}_X(z)\), is a
40	topological quantity attached to the specialisation preorder on the underlying
41	topological space of \(X\): the supremum of the lengths of strict-generisation
42	chains \(z = a_0 \prec a_1 \prec \cdots \prec a_n\) in \(X\), where \(\prec\) is the
43	strict specialisation order \(x \prec y \iff y \rightsquigarrow x \wedge y \neq x\).
44	
45	These two invariants agree:
46	\[
47	  \dim \mathcal{O}_{X,z} \;=\; \operatorname{coheight}_X(z),
48	\]
49	which is the headline theorem \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight} of this
50	chapter. The identification is folklore in algebraic geometry --- it is the
51	statement that the dimension of the local ring at \(z\) counts irreducible
52	closed subsets of \(X\) containing \(z\) --- but at the project's pinned Mathlib
53	commit (\texttt{b80f227}) the equation is not packaged as a single declaration
54	and must be assembled project-side from four Mathlib API pieces.
55	
56	\paragraph{Why the bridge is needed.} Two downstream files in the project
57	currently work around the missing bridge by threading explicit
58	\texttt{[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk \_)]} instance arguments at every
59	codim-\(1\) use site:
60	\begin{itemize}
61	  \item \cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension} carries an internal conjunction
62	    \[
63	      \text{hreg\_dim : IsRegularLocalRing\ } \mathcal{O}_{X,z} \;\wedge\;
64	      \operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) = 1
65	    \]
66	    inside the private helper
67	    \texttt{smooth\_codim\_one\_maximalIdeal\_isPrincipal\_and\_ne\_bot};
68	    the Krull-dim conjunct is supplied by \emph{this chapter's}
69	    \cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one}, leaving only the
70	    irreducible \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing} half (which is the separate
71	    Stacks tag \texttt{00TT} sub-project, out of scope here).
72	  \item \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}'s order-at-a-prime-divisor map
73	    \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.order} currently carries an explicit
74	    \texttt{[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]} instance argument;
75	    after \cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one} lands, the instance is
76	    synthesisable from the codim-\(1\) hypothesis on \(Y\) alone, so the explicit
77	    argument can be dropped.
78	\end{itemize}
79	
80	\paragraph{Chapter structure.} \cref{sec:coheight_topology} proves the
81	topology-only lemma that coheight is preserved by open immersions
82	(\cref{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}). \cref{sec:coheight_spec_duality}
83	identifies coheight on a \(\Spec\) with the height of the underlying prime ideal
84	(\cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}). \cref{sec:coheight_bridge}
85	assembles the bridge \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}. The closing
86	\cref{sec:coheight_le_one_instance} packages the codim-\(1\) specialisation
87	\cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one} that downstream files consume.
88	
89	\section{Coheight is preserved by open immersions}
90	\label{sec:coheight_topology}
91	
92	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2 sketch
93	% L246--257 (the recipe extracts the lemma from a generic
94	% \texttt{Specializes.mem\_open} chain-lifting argument). The lemma is a
95	% generic topological fact about the specialisation preorder; no external
96	% reference shipped at \texttt{b80f227} states it under this exact
97	% formulation.
98	
99	\begin{lemma}
100	
101	\leanok
102	  [Coheight on opens equals coheight in the ambient space]
103	  \label{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}
104	  \lean{Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}
105	  Let \(X\) be a topological space carrying the specialisation preorder, and
106	  let \(U \subseteq X\) be an open subset. For any \(z \in U\),
107	  \[
108	    \operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) \;=\; \operatorname{coheight}_{U}\bigl(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\bigr),
109	  \]
110	  where on the right \(U\) carries the subspace topology and $\langle z, \,
111	  z \in U\rangle\( denotes \)z\( viewed as a point of \)U$.
112	\end{lemma}
113	
114	\begin{proof}
115	
116	\leanok
117	  Both sides are by definition the supremum, over natural numbers \(n\), of the
118	  set of \(n\) for which there exists a strictly increasing chain
119	  \(z = a_0 \prec a_1 \prec \cdots \prec a_n\) in the relevant
120	  specialisation preorder (with \(\prec\) denoting strict specialisation). It
121	  therefore suffices to give an order-preserving bijection between the
122	  strict-generisation chains starting at \(z\) in \(X\) and those starting at the
123	  corresponding point of \(U\).
124	
125	  \emph{Chains in \(U\) embed into \(X\).} The inclusion \(U \hookrightarrow X\) is
126	  continuous, so it preserves specialisation: if \(y \rightsquigarrow x\) in \(U\)
127	  then \(y \rightsquigarrow x\) in \(X\), and distinct points of \(U\) remain
128	  distinct in \(X\). Hence every strict-generisation chain in \(U\) starting at
129	  \(z\) lifts to one in \(X\) of the same length.
130	
131	  \emph{Chains in \(X\) starting at \(z\) all lie in \(U\).} Conversely, suppose
132	  \(z = a_0 \prec a_1 \prec \cdots \prec a_n\) is a strict-generisation chain
133	  in \(X\) with \(a_0 = z \in U\). For each \(i \geq 0\), the relation $a_i \succeq
134	  z\( (in the specialisation preorder) is \)z \rightsquigarrow a_i$, i.e.\
135	  \(a_i\) is in the closure of \(\{z\}\) \emph{viewed from above}; in our
136	  convention the strict order \(a \prec b\) records \(b \rightsquigarrow a\)
137	  (the generic point is the strict maximum), so \(a_i \succeq z\) in \(X\) means
138	  \(a_i \rightsquigarrow z\) in \(X\). By the Mathlib lemma
139	  \texttt{Topology.Inseparable.Specializes.mem\_open} (any specialisation
140	  \(a \rightsquigarrow z\) with \(z\) in an open set \(U\) forces \(a \in U\), since
141	  \(U\) is open and contains \(z\), hence contains every point whose closure
142	  contains \(z\) --- equivalently every \(a\) with \(a \rightsquigarrow z\)),
143	  every \(a_i\) lies in \(U\). Thus the chain descends to a strict-generisation
144	  chain $\langle z\rangle = \langle a_0\rangle \prec \langle a_1\rangle \prec
145	  \cdots \prec \langle a_n\rangle\( in \)U$ of the same length.
146	
147	  These two operations are mutually inverse on the set of strict-generisation
148	  chains starting at \(z\) (resp.\ at \(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\)) and
149	  preserve length, so the suprema agree.
150	\end{proof}
151	
152	\section{Coheight on Spec equals height in PrimeSpectrum}
153	\label{sec:coheight_spec_duality}
154	
155	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2 sketch
156	% L259--268. The convention reconciliation step traces to Mathlib's
157	% \texttt{spec\_le\_iff} in \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace} L438--447
158	% and to \texttt{Order.coheight\_orderIso} +
159	% \texttt{Order.height\_toDual} in \texttt{Mathlib.Order.KrullDimension}.
160	
161	\begin{lemma}
162	
163	\leanok
164	  [Coheight on \(\Spec R\) equals height in \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum} R\)]
165	  \label{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}
166	  \lean{Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}
167	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring and let \(p \in \Spec R\) (viewed as a point of
168	  the scheme spectrum, with the specialisation preorder). Then
169	  \[
170	    \operatorname{coheight}_{\Spec R}(p) \;=\; \operatorname{height}_{\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)}(p),
171	  \]
172	  where the right-hand side is the supremum of the lengths of strict
173	  prime-ideal chains \(\mathfrak{p}_0 \subsetneq \cdots \subsetneq \mathfrak{p}\)
174	  ending at the prime \(\mathfrak{p}\) corresponding to \(p\).
175	\end{lemma}
176	
177	\begin{proof}
178	
179	\leanok
180	  The specialisation preorder on \(\Spec R\) is the dual of the natural
181	  inclusion preorder on \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)\). Concretely, for
182	  \(p, q \in \Spec R\) with underlying primes \(\mathfrak{p}, \mathfrak{q}\),
183	  the relation \(p \leq q\) in the specialisation preorder (which is
184	  \(q \rightsquigarrow p\) in topological notation) corresponds to the prime
185	  containment \(\mathfrak{q} \subseteq \mathfrak{p}\). This is the content of
186	  the Mathlib lemma \texttt{spec\_le\_iff} (located in
187	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace}, lines 438--447 at commit
188	  \texttt{b80f227}, with the explicit remark that the preorder on \(\Spec R\)
189	  equals the order-dual of the preorder on \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum} R\)).
190	
191	  Under this order anti-isomorphism, coheight of \(p\) in \(\Spec R\)
192	  (= supremum of strict-generisation chain lengths starting at \(p\),
193	  i.e.\ ascending chains \(p \prec a_1 \prec \cdots \prec a_n\) in the
194	  specialisation preorder) translates to height of \(\mathfrak{p}\) in
195	  \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)\) (= supremum of strict descending prime-ideal
196	  chains $\mathfrak{p} \supsetneq \mathfrak{q}_1 \supsetneq \cdots \supsetneq
197	  \mathfrak{q}_n$, equivalently strict ascending chains
198	  $\mathfrak{q}_n \subsetneq \cdots \subsetneq \mathfrak{q}_1 \subsetneq
199	  \mathfrak{p}$). The transport is mechanised by the Mathlib lemmas
200	  \texttt{Order.coheight\_orderIso} (coheight is invariant under order
201	  isomorphisms) and \texttt{Order.height\_toDual} (height in the order-dual
202	  equals coheight in the original order), applied to the order-dual
203	  identification \(\Spec R \simeq_o (\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum} R)^{\operatorname{op}}\)
204	  furnished by \texttt{spec\_le\_iff}.
205	\end{proof}
206	
207	\section{The coheight \(=\) Krull-dim-of-stalk bridge}
208	\label{sec:coheight_bridge}
209	
210	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2 sketch
211	% L280--296 (the 5-step assembly recipe). Mathlib API surface consumed:
212	%   - \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.exists\_isAffineOpen\_mem\_and\_subset}
213	%     (\texttt{AffineScheme.lean:271})
214	%   - \texttt{IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf}, \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk}
215	%     (\texttt{AffineScheme.lean:806--813})
216	%   - \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}
217	%     (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Height:341--348})
218	%   - \texttt{Ideal.height\_eq\_primeHeight}
219	%     (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Height:45})
220	%   - \cref{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}, \cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}
221	
222	\begin{theorem}
223	
224	\leanok
225	  [Coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge for a scheme point]
226	  \label{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}
227	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}
228	  \uses{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding, lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}
229	
230	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(z \in X\) be any point. Then
231	  \[
232	    \operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) \;=\; \operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z),
233	  \]
234	  i.e.\ the Krull dimension of the stalk at \(z\) equals the coheight of \(z\) in
235	  the underlying topological space of \(X\) (with respect to the specialisation
236	  preorder).
237	\end{theorem}
238	
239	\begin{proof}
240	  
241	  \leanok
242	  The proof proceeds in five steps, assembling Mathlib API rather than
243	  arguing from a single textbook reference.
244	
245	  \emph{Step 1: pick an affine open neighbourhood of \(z\).} By
246	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.exists\_isAffineOpen\_mem\_and\_subset}
247	  applied to the open set \(X\) itself, there exists an affine open
248	  \(U \subseteq X\) with \(z \in U\). Write \(\langle z, \, z \in U \rangle \in U\)
249	  for \(z\) viewed as a point of the subscheme \(U\).
250	
251	  \emph{Step 2: name the prime corresponding to \(z\) inside \(U\).} Since
252	  \(U \cong \Spec \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)\) via the affine equivalence
253	  \(U \simeq \Spec R\) (with \(R := \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)\)), the point
254	  \(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\) corresponds to a prime ideal $\mathfrak{p}
255	  \subseteq R$. In Mathlib this prime is named
256	  \texttt{IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf}\,\(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\).
257	
258	  \emph{Step 3: the stalk is the localisation of \(\Gamma(U)\) at
259	  \(\mathfrak{p}\).} The classical affine-stalk identification says
260	  \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z} \cong R_{\mathfrak{p}}\). In Mathlib this is the
261	  instance \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk}, which witnesses
262	  \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\) as a localisation of \(R\) at \(\mathfrak{p}\) in the
263	  \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} sense.
264	
265	  \emph{Step 4: Krull dim of the localisation equals the height of
266	  \(\mathfrak{p}\).} The Mathlib lemma
267	  \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height} gives
268	  \[
269	    \operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) \;=\;
270	    \operatorname{ringKrullDim}(R_{\mathfrak{p}}) \;=\;
271	    \operatorname{height}(\mathfrak{p}),
272	  \]
273	  where \(\operatorname{height}(\mathfrak{p})\) is the prime-ideal height in
274	  \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)\). By
275	  \texttt{Ideal.height\_eq\_primeHeight} this matches the
276	  \(\operatorname{height}_{\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)}\) used in
277	  \cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}.
278	
279	  \emph{Step 5: lift back from \(U\) to \(X\).} By
280	  \cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum} applied to \(R\) and the
281	  affine identification \(U \simeq \Spec R\),
282	  \[
283	    \operatorname{height}_{\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum}(R)}(\mathfrak{p}) \;=\;
284	    \operatorname{coheight}_{\Spec R}(\langle\text{image of } z\rangle) \;=\;
285	    \operatorname{coheight}_{U}\bigl(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\bigr).
286	  \]
287	  Then \cref{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding} applied to the open
288	  \(U \subseteq X\) promotes the right-hand side back to
289	  \(\operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z)\). Concatenating the equalities from
290	  Steps~4--5 gives the conclusion.
291	\end{proof}
292	
293	\section{Codim-1 specialisation: synthesising \texorpdfstring{\(\operatorname{KrullDimLE} 1\)}{KrullDimLE 1}}
294	\label{sec:coheight_le_one_instance}
295	
296	% Source pointer: \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 2
297	% recipe scaffold L298--307 + Decision 2 hreg\_dim refactor sketch
298	% L319--340. This block is the consumer-facing wrapper of the bridge
299	% \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight} for the codim-\(1\) case used in
300	% \cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension} and
301	% \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}.
302	
303	\begin{lemma}
304	
305	\leanok
306	  [Coheight \(= 1\) forces stalk \(\operatorname{Krull\,dim} \leq 1\)]
307	  \label{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one}
308	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one}
309	  \uses{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}
310	
311	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(z \in X\) be a point with
312	  \(\operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) = 1\). Then the stalk \(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\)
313	  has Krull dimension at most \(1\), equivalently $\operatorname{Ring.KrullDimLE}\,1\,
314	  \mathcal{O}_{X,z}$ holds.
315	\end{lemma}
316	
317	\begin{proof}
318	  
319	  \leanok
320	  By \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight},
321	  \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) = \operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) = 1\).
322	  The Mathlib predicate \(\operatorname{Ring.KrullDimLE}\,1\,R\) unfolds to
323	  \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(R) \leq 1\), so the inequality \(1 \leq 1\)
324	  discharges the goal.
325	\end{proof}
326	
327	\section{Consumer wiring and the Stacks 00TT carve-out}
328	\label{sec:coheight_consumers}
329	
330	\paragraph{Consumer 1: the \texttt{hreg\_dim} refactor in
331	\texorpdfstring{\cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension}}{the codim-1 extension chapter}.}
332	The private helper
333	\texttt{smooth\_codim\_one\_maximalIdeal\_isPrincipal\_and\_ne\_bot}
334	inside that chapter's Lean target currently carries an internal
335	\texttt{sorry} of the form
336	\[
337	  \text{hreg\_dim : IsRegularLocalRing}\,\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\;\wedge\;
338	  \operatorname{ringKrullDim}\,\mathcal{O}_{X,z} = 1.
339	\]
340	After this chapter's
341	\cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one} (and its underlying
342	\cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}) land, the conjunction splits into
343	two independent goals:
344	\begin{itemize}
345	  \item The right conjunct \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(\mathcal{O}_{X,z}) = 1\)
346	    is discharged by
347	    \(\operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) = 1\) (the standing codim-\(1\) hypothesis
348	    on \(z\)) plus \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}; the antisymmetric
349	    pair \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim} \leq 1\) (\cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one})
350	    and \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim} \geq 1\) (also from the bridge, since
351	    \(1 \leq 1\)) gives equality.
352	  \item The left conjunct \(\operatorname{IsRegularLocalRing}\,\mathcal{O}_{X,z}\)
353	    is the genuinely-irreducible \emph{Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}} statement
354	    ``smooth ring map over a field forces regular stalks''. It is a separate,
355	    out-of-scope sub-project (Mathlib does \emph{not} ship the implication
356	    \texttt{Algebra.Smooth k A \(\to\) IsRegularLocalRing \(A_{\mathfrak{p}}\)} at
357	    \texttt{b80f227}; the project-side build is \(\sim 200\)--\(300\)~LOC of
358	    cotangent-space and pure-dimension machinery, not packaged in this
359	    chapter).
360	\end{itemize}
361	Thus the present chapter \emph{halves} the \texttt{hreg\_dim} obligation in
362	\cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension}: one conjunct closes via the bridge,
363	one remains as a named Mathlib gap.
364	
365	\paragraph{Consumer 2: the \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.order} threading
366	hygiene in \texorpdfstring{\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}}{the Weil-divisor chapter}.}
367	The order-at-a-prime-divisor map currently carries an explicit instance
368	argument
369	\[
370	  [\,\operatorname{Ring.KrullDimLE}\,1\,(X.\text{presheaf.stalk}\,Y.\text{point})\,]
371	\]
372	at every use site. Because \(Y\) is a prime divisor of \(X\) (so
373	\(\operatorname{coheight}_{X}(Y.\text{point}) = 1\) by the definition of a prime
374	divisor as a codim-\(1\) irreducible closed subset),
375	\cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one} synthesises the instance
376	automatically from the codim-\(1\) hypothesis on \(Y\), so the explicit instance
377	argument can be dropped from \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.order}'s signature.
378	
379	\paragraph{Consumer 3: future codim-\(1\) infrastructure.} Any subsequent
380	project lemma that needs to discharge ``the stalk at a codim-\(1\) point has
381	Krull dim \(\leq 1\)'' --- the implicit hypothesis behind every DVR-at-codim-\(1\)
382	argument (cf.\ \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} of
383	\cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension}) --- now has a one-liner consequence.
384	The instance form
385	\cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one} is the right shape for
386	typeclass-search to find it without ceremony.
387	
388	\section{Lean encoding}
389	\label{sec:coheight_lean_encoding}
390	
391	The Lean target file is the new
392	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean}. The declarations to
393	introduce, in order:
394	
395	\begin{enumerate}
396	  \item \texttt{Order.coheight\_eq\_of\_isOpenEmbedding} ---
397	    \cref{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding}. Signature (informal): for
398	    \(X\) a topological space, \(U \subseteq X\) open, and \(z \in U\),
399	    \(\operatorname{coheight}_{X}(z) = \operatorname{coheight}_{U}(\langle z, hz\rangle)\).
400	    Tools: \texttt{Topology.Inseparable.Specializes.mem\_open} for the
401	    chain-lifting direction; \texttt{Subtype.val} continuity for the
402	    embedding direction. Estimated \(\sim 20\)--\(30\)~LOC.
403	  \item \texttt{Order.coheight\_spec\_eq\_height\_primeSpectrum} ---
404	    \cref{lem:coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum}. Signature (informal):
405	    for \(R\) a \texttt{CommRingCat} and \(p : \Spec R\) a point, coheight of \(p\)
406	    in \(\Spec R\) equals height in \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum} R\). Tools:
407	    \texttt{spec\_le\_iff} (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace} L438--447),
408	    \texttt{Order.coheight\_orderIso}, \texttt{Order.height\_toDual}
409	    (\texttt{Mathlib.Order.KrullDimension}). Estimated \(\sim 10\)--\(15\)~LOC.
410	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim\_stalk\_eq\_coheight} ---
411	    \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight}. Signature (informal): for
412	    \(X : \texttt{Scheme}\) and \(z : X\),
413	    $\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(X.\text{presheaf.stalk}\,z) =
414	    \operatorname{coheight}\,z$. Five-step assembly per the proof above.
415	    Tools: \texttt{exists\_isAffineOpen\_mem\_and\_subset},
416	    \texttt{IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf}, \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk},
417	    \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height},
418	    \texttt{Ideal.height\_eq\_primeHeight}, plus the two lemmas above.
419	    Estimated \(\sim 30\)--\(50\)~LOC.
420	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDimLE\_of\_coheight\_eq\_one} ---
421	    \cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one}. Signature (informal):
422	    given \(X : \texttt{Scheme}\), \(z : X\), and \(\operatorname{coheight}\,z = 1\),
423	    derive \texttt{Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z)}. Trivial corollary
424	    of (3). Estimated \(\sim 10\)~LOC. Lemma vs.\ instance phrasing: the
425	    \texttt{coheight\,z = 1} hypothesis is not a typeclass, so this lands as
426	    a plain lemma. Downstream files that have the codim-\(1\) hypothesis in
427	    hand invoke it explicitly; downstream typeclass infrastructure that
428	    bundles ``\(z\) is a codim-\(1\) point'' into a typeclass (if any is added
429	    later) can re-export as an instance.
430	\end{enumerate}
431	
432	\paragraph{Mathlib readiness audit.} At commit \texttt{b80f227} the prover
433	should verify:
434	\begin{itemize}
435	  \item \texttt{Order.coheight}, \texttt{Order.height}, \texttt{Order.coheight\_orderIso},
436	    \texttt{Order.height\_toDual} all live in
437	    \texttt{Mathlib.Order.KrullDimension} and are imported transitively by
438	    \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Stalk}.
439	  \item \texttt{Topology.Inseparable.Specializes.mem\_open} lives in
440	    \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Inseparable}; the file already imports it via
441	    \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Defs.Filter}.
442	  \item \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk} and
443	    \texttt{IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf} live in
444	    \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme} (lines 806--813 and
445	    surrounding, at the pinned commit).
446	  \item \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height} and
447	    \texttt{Ideal.height\_eq\_primeHeight} live in
448	    \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Height} (lines 341--348 and 45 at the
449	    pinned commit).
450	  \item \texttt{Ring.KrullDimLE} is defined and unfolds to
451	    \(\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(R) \leq n\) at \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Basic}.
452	\end{itemize}
453	No new core axiom is required. The chapter introduces no new macro.
454	
455	\section{Out of scope}
456	\label{sec:coheight_out_of_scope}
457	
458	This chapter packages \emph{only} the coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge and its
459	codim-\(1\) specialisation. The following adjacent content is explicitly out
460	of scope:
461	\begin{itemize}
462	  \item \textbf{Stacks tag \texttt{00TT} (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) regular stalks).}
463	    This is a separate sub-project of \(\sim 200\)--\(300\)~LOC, tracked in
464	    \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 1. The directive
465	    flags it as iter-200+ work. The natural project landing site, when
466	    attacked, is a standalone file
467	    \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/SmoothToRegular.lean} (not part of
468	    this chapter).
469	  \item \textbf{The codim-\(1\) valuative criterion / Milne Lemma 3.3
470	    difference-map construction.} This is the second multi-iter sub-project
471	    flagged in \texttt{analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md} Decision 3
472	    (\(\sim 300\)--\(500\)~LOC of project-side new infrastructure); see
473	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} in
474	    \cref{chap:Albanese_CodimOneExtension} for the chapter that owns it.
475	  \item \textbf{Refactoring \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.order} to drop its
476	    explicit \texttt{[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 \_]} instance argument.} This is a
477	    downstream consequence of \cref{lem:ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one}
478	    that lives in \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}; the present chapter
479	    only \emph{enables} the refactor, it does not perform it.
480	  \item \textbf{Mathlib upstream PRs.} The two lemmas
481	    \cref{lem:coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding} and
482	    \cref{thm:ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight} are natural candidates for
483	    upstreaming (their natural Mathlib homes are
484	    \texttt{Mathlib.Order.KrullDimension} and a new
485	    \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Coheight} respectively); the chapter
486	    flags this as an observation but does not pursue it.
487	  \item \textbf{General codim-\(n\) specialisations.} Only the codim-\(1\)
488	    instance is packaged here, because that is the consumer-facing case.
489	    A general \texttt{ringKrullDimLE\_of\_coheight\_le} or
490	    \texttt{ringKrullDimLE\_of\_coheight\_eq} family of lemmas can be added
491	    iteratively if downstream demand emerges, but is not required for any
492	    A.4.* sub-row at the time of writing.
493	\end{itemize}

# Render-cleanup report — Albanese_CoheightBridge.tex

## Defect class: `math-delim` (interleaved / `$...$`-vs-`\(...\)` normalisation)

Swept the whole file. All 8 `$...$` sites flagged by the doctor were normalised to
`\(...\)` math with prose kept outside the math delimiters. No statement text, `\lean{}`,
`\label{}`, or `\uses{}` semantics were touched; only prose/proof delimiters changed.

### Edits (line → defect → before → after)

1. **L110–111** — interleaved (inverted `$MATH\( prose \)MATH$`). 
   Before: `$\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\( denotes \)z\( viewed as a point of \)U$`
   After: `\(\langle z, \, z \in U\rangle\) denotes \(z\) viewed as a point of \(U\)`

2. **L133–134** — interleaved. 
   Before: `$a_i \succeq z\( (in the specialisation preorder) is \)z \rightsquigarrow a_i$`
   After: `\(a_i \succeq z\) (in the specialisation preorder) is \(z \rightsquigarrow a_i\)`

3. **L144–145** — interleaved. 
   Before: `$\langle z\rangle = \langle a_0\rangle \prec \cdots \prec \langle a_n\rangle\( in \)U$`
   After: `\(\langle z\rangle = \langle a_0\rangle \prec \cdots \prec \langle a_n\rangle\) in \(U\)`

4. **L196–199** — plain `$...$` flagged on lines also carrying `\(...\)` (two formulae). 
   Before: `$\mathfrak{p} \supsetneq \cdots \supsetneq \mathfrak{q}_n$ ... $\mathfrak{q}_n \subsetneq \cdots \subsetneq \mathfrak{p}$`
   After: `\(\mathfrak{p} \supsetneq \cdots \supsetneq \mathfrak{q}_n\) ... \(\mathfrak{q}_n \subsetneq \cdots \subsetneq \mathfrak{p}\)`

5. **L254–255** — plain `$...$`. 
   Before: `$\mathfrak{p} \subseteq R$`
   After: `\(\mathfrak{p} \subseteq R\)`

6. **L313–314** — plain `$...$`. 
   Before: `$\operatorname{Ring.KrullDimLE}\,1\, \mathcal{O}_{X,z}$`
   After: `\(\operatorname{Ring.KrullDimLE}\,1\, \mathcal{O}_{X,z}\)`

7. **L413–414** — plain `$...$`. 
   Before: `$\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(X.\text{presheaf.stalk}\,z) = \operatorname{coheight}\,z$`
   After: `\(\operatorname{ringKrullDim}(X.\text{presheaf.stalk}\,z) = \operatorname{coheight}\,z\)`

## Verification

- `grep '\$'` → none remaining.
- `grep 'REF'` → none (no `literal-ref` defects in this chapter, as expected).
- Interleave check (`$` adjacent to `\(`/`\)`) → none remaining.
- No `\cref{}` introduced (no literal-ref/bare-label to repair).
- No statement text, `\lean{}`, `\label{}`, `\uses{}`, `\leanok`, or block ordering changed.

## Unresolved

None. All flagged `math-delim` sites resolved.
